title: Gochnatia picardae
intro: Gochnatia picardae, also known as "Pico's tree daisy," is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and woody stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Gochnatia picardae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Gochnatia picardae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Galapagos Islands. The classification of Gochnatia picardae places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Its family classification in Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, groups it with other flowering plants that produce daisy-like flowers. Further classification within the genus Gochnatia positions it as a member of the Gochnatieae tribe.
